item.title: Research-informed coherence in chemistry teacher education: PCK, inquiry, and student agency across aligned curriculum
item.description: This paper analyses the chemistry teacher education pathway at the University of Jyväskylä, examining how its course architecture systematically develops pedagogical content knowledge, inquiry‑ and phenomenon‑based pedagogy, student agency, and assessment ...

item.title: Microsoft Skala’s One-Electron Performance: Self-Interaction Error in a Machine-Learned XC Functional
item.description: Benchmarking the machine-learned Microsoft Skala functional against H+ 2 dissociation reveals significant improvements regarding self-interaction error (SIE). Unlike Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof (PBE), which exhibits severe SIE, Skala substantially reduces this ...

item.title: ROBUST TRANSFER LEARNING FOR BAYESIAN OPTIMIZATION OF CHEMICAL REACTIONS
item.description: Transfer learning offers a promising route to accelerate Bayesian optimization (BO) by reusing knowledge acquired during related optimization tasks. In chemical reaction optimization, this most naturally arises during search-space expansion, where ...

item.title: Topology-Driven Molecular Recognition through Constitutional Isomerism
item.description: Constitutional isomerism is among the most fundamental forms of molecular structural variation, yet how it governs molecular recognition across multiple physicochemical scales remains largely unexplored. Here we establish a multiscale computational ...

item.title: Reactivity of Water Dimer Radical Cations toward Inert Molecules: DFT Approaches for the Realistic Calculation of Ground- and Excited-State Reaction Profiles
item.description: For the last quarter of century, the topic of chemistry of radical cations (RACs) is expanding at an extreme rate. Produced via single-electron transfer by photo-redox or electrochemical methods, RACs attract attention of organic chemists due to the ...

item.title: Computational Discovery of Antimalarial Candidates from African Natural Product Chemical Space
item.description: Virtual screening of antimalarial targets typically requires tens of thousands of docking runs per target, a computational cost that restricts discovery in resourcelimited endemic regions. We developed a centroid-based screening framework that combines ...

item.title: Advancing polypharmacology-based drug discovery using deep generative models
SmartReact: An Open-Source Python Package for Scalable Forward Synthesis Enumeration Built on Interpretable SMARTS Templates
item.description: Predicting which products can be made from a set of building blocks underpins library design, analogue generation, and synthesis-constrained generative modelling. Reaction templates encoded as SMARTS patterns are the standard tool for this forward ...

item.title: Stochastic Evaluation of Exciton–Exciton Annihilation Rate in 2D Molecular Aggregate
item.description: Exciton-exciton annihilation (EEA) is a process by which two excitons combine to form a single high energy excitation. In molecular chromophore aggregates EEA is governed by microscopic two-exciton coherence that depends on long-range coupling and ...

item.title: CoreMeta4Cat: Guidelines for Metadata Standards in Catalysis
item.description: Catalysis is a key technological pillar of the global chemical industry, enabling energy-efficient, sustainable and climate-friendly chemical production. Despite the maturity of the field, the reproducibility, interoperability and reuse of experimental ...

item.title: Electronic Energy Migration in Protein Polymers Associated with Neurodegenerative Diseases
item.description: Neurodegenerative diseases are primarily characterized by the accumulation of misfolded protein aggregates in the brain. In addition to their pathological roles, these aggregates have been proposed to transduce photochemical stimuli into biochemical ...

item.title: Direct solvothermal growth of TaS2 on substrates enables stable self-supported hydrogen evolution electrocatalysts
item.description: Layered transition metal sulfides, such as TaS2, have emerged as promising electrocatalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER). However, the solution-phase synthesis of TaS2 remains challenging and underdeveloped. Herein, we present an effective ...

item.title: Physics-guided neural networks for pure-liquid viscosity prediction: Controlling structural and temperature extrapolation through derivative regularization
item.description: Data-driven property models support chemical process and molecular design, yet reliable prediction remains difficult beyond sampled chemical and operating-condition domains. Using pure-component viscosity, we diagnosed structural-and temperature-...

item.title: Nonthermal Plasma-Assisted Methane Dehydroaromatization to Alkyl Aromatics
item.description: The non-oxidative conversion of methane to aromatics is an attractive alternative to conventional petroleum reforming, yet practical implementation remains hindered by thermodynamic constraints, severe coke formation, and rapid catalyst deactivation at ...

item.title: AtomFormer: A Simple Model for Any Mixture Odor Prediction and Beyond
item.description: *Corresponding author: yangxiao_ztri@foxmail.com Abstract. Odor prediction has relied on two implicit assumptions: that a closed set of expert-defined labels is necessary for training, and that individual molecules must serve as the encoding unit—forcing ...

item.title: Objective-Dependent Biasing of Markov Networks for Relaxation and First Passage
item.description: Enhanced-sampling simulations accelerate rare molecular transitions by adding a bias potential, but a bias that accelerates one kinetic quantity need not accelerate another, and a strong bias can reshape the transition pathway it is meant to characterize. ...

item.description: Hydrazone-based molecular photoswitches have emerged as one of the most promising classes of light-responsive systems, particularly owing to their straightforward synthesis and broad structural tunability. However, only a limited number of hydrazone ...

item.description: Aza-diarylethenes (aza-DAEs) are an emerging class of molecular photoswitches that allow reversible C-N bond formation and cleaving upon irradiation. They show not only high potential as T-type photochromes but offer exciting opportunities for the ...

item.title: De Novo Design of Enantioselective Artificial Metalloenzyme Photocatalysts Containing Metal Polypyridine Cofactors
item.description: The ability to create protein scaffolds that impart enantioselectivity to simple, readily available metal catalysts could redefine the landscape of asymmetric catalysis. Artificial metalloenzymes (ArMs), hybrid catalysts that combine a synthetic metal ...

item.title: From One-Dimensional Chains to Single-Crystal Nanoelectronic Devices: Linking Structure, Band Energetics and Ambipolar Transport in an Ammonia-Based Magnus-type Salt
item.description: Ammonia-based Magnus-type salts have long been recognized as highly insoluble coordination compounds whose one-dimensional chain structures give rise to unusual optical and electronic properties. Despite nearly two centuries of study and persistent debate,...

item.title: Synthesis and adsorption at the air/solution interface of new pH-responsive dicephalic and quadruple-head cationic surfactants

item.title: Near-Unity Sodium Metal Cycling without an Anion-Rich Interphase
item.description: Sodium metal anodes promise high energy density at low cost, yet they show poor reversibility in common carbonate electrolytes. The prevailing design strategy, inherited from lithium metal research, holds that reversibility requires a dense, anion-derived,...

item.title: In Silico Screening of Drug-Like Compounds Against the TcBDF2 Bromodomain of Trypanosoma cruzi: Identification of Potential Inhibitors for Chagas Disease Treatment

item.description: The spatial organization of guest molecules within metal-organic frameworks strongly influence adsorption, transport, separation, and reactivity, yet controlling guest location across chemically and structurally distinct pore environments remains ...

item.title: Experimental molecular assembly estimations for space exploration using gas chromatography mass spectrometry
item.description: Most biosignature methods are biased by what we know about life on Earth. However, an agnostic approach would be more beneficial when looking for life on other planets. Molecular Assembly (MA) is an experimental agnostic biosignature method that could ...

item.description: Coarse-grained (CG) simulations efficiently sample the mesoscale morphologies that govern charge, ion, and exciton transport in soft electronic materials, but they integrate out the atomistic degrees of freedom needed to evaluate electronic properties. ...

item.description: Molecular glue induced targeted protein degradation is a powerful emerging therapeutic modality that exploits the ubiquitin-proteasome degradation pathway to break down proteins associated with disease proliferation. At the structural level, degradation ...

item.title: Potential for off-grid flexible production of sustainable aviation fuel in Australia
item.description: offers a pathway to decarbonise the aviation sector, but off-grid systems face fundamental challenges from the intermittency of solar and wind resources. This paper develops an optimisation framework for off-grid e-SAF production integrating solar and ...

item.description: Regarding the essential issue of the oscillator in Planck's blackbody thermal radiation, the free electron model (FEM) in potential box has been proposed. The results of nonlinear curve fitting have shown that FEM can describe the intensity distribution ...

item.title: PAMOS: Encoding Chemical Environment Response in a Minimal Basis Set Molecular Orbital Method. I. One-Electron Diatomic Ions
item.description: We present PAMOS (Parameterized Approximate Molecular Orbital method with Slater orbitals), a semiempirical electronic structure method that keeps a minimal Slater-type basis set yet captures the response of atomic orbitals to their molecular environment. ...

item.title: Collision Induced Unfolding Predicts the Aggregation of Glycoengineered Monoclonal Antibodies
item.description: Monoclonal antibody (mAb) therapeutics exhibit substantial structural heterogeneity, requiring analytical methods capable of rapidly assessing critical quality attributes (CQAs) that influence stability and developability. Non-native aggregation is a key ...

item.title: Efficient and Accurate Ligand Strain Calculations in Solution with the AIMNet2 Neural Network Potential
item.description: Conformational strain, the energy a ligand pays to adopt its bound conformation, contributes to binding affinity but is routinely misestimated. The standard estimate references a single gasphase energy minimum, which ignores solvent and mishandles charged ...

item.title: SurfPro2: a relational database, curation workflow and repository of surfactant physicochemical data
item.description: Access to diverse, well-curated experimental data remains a major challenge in chemistry, underpinning applications ranging from machine learning (ML) and molecular discovery to data-driven scientific understanding. While several repositories provide ...

item.title: Foundation interatomic potentials learn the practical chemistry they were never taught
item.description: Chemists understand molecular reactivity through simplified concepts such as substituent effects, acidity, and aromaticity, which are compact descriptors of the potential energy surface and of the electronic structure that shapes it. Universal machine-...

item.title: Discrimination and Classification of Coke Variants via Hybrid E-tongue
item.description: Soft drinks contain different proportions of sugars, salts and other compounds, giving them a signature taste. We explore whether hybrid e-tongue (HET), utilized previously to discriminate pure tastes, can discern the tastes of Original (OC), Diet (DC) ...

item.title: Strategies Toward Pharmaceutical Self-reliance in Nepal for Strengthening the Health Care System.
item.description: Under the federal system of governance in Nepal, health facilities are supervised by the local and provincial governments. However, people have faced challenges in accessing quality, affordable medicines over the years. Lack of innovation and technology, ...

item.title: Comparing Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory to Intermolecular Energy Decomposition Analysis Based on Absolutely Localized Molecular Orbitals
Synthordle: A web-based game for retrosynthetic training
item.description: Organic chemistry courses often suffer from high attrition and may benefit from instructional approaches beyond passive recall. Retrosynthetic analysis, a core skill for organic chemists, requires iterative practice and flexible reasoning rather than rote ...

item.title: Sustainable Ammonia Production from Biogas: A Review
item.description: Green hydrogen produced from biogas reforming offers a low-carbon feedstock route to sustainable ammonia synthesis, coupling waste valorization with decarbonized fertilizer and chemical production. This review examines the technical foundations of this ...

item.title: Modulation of Open-Shell Character in Dibenzoazaborine Dications through Amino-Substituent Modification
item.description: Oxidation of dibutylamino-substituted dibenzoazaborine Bu2NDBAB afforded the air-stable dication [Bu2NDBAB](SbF6)2 in 83% yield, with an NIR absorption band at 943 nm. Single-crystal X-ray diffraction revealed pronounced quinoidal distortion. Unlike the ...

item.title: A Curated 12,597-Structure MOF Library and Geometric Pre-Screening Protocol for CO2/N2/H2O Co-Adsorption
item.description: Pore size, pore shape, and framework density are among the most direct chemical handles available for tuning a metal–organic framework's (MOF's) affinity for CO2 over N2 and H2O, yet these structural descriptors are rarely used systematically to narrow a ...

item.title: Advancing polypharmacology-based drug discovery using deep generative models
Catalyst-Aware Regression for Oxidative Coupling of Methane: A Reproducible Small-Data Baseline
item.description: Oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) performance depends on both operating conditions and catalyst formulation. This study evaluates whether explicit catalyst identity improves prediction of C2 yield in a small experimental dataset containing 291 OCM runs. ...

item.title: A Chemist in the Loop: Deploying VLM Agents for Reaction Conditions Optimization Towards Manufacturing
item.description: The optimization of reaction conditions to max-imize product yield is a critical bottleneck in chemical innovation, where every additional iteration, in the lab or the manufacturing plant, adds to costs of reagents, time, and expert effort. We report the ...

item.title: Pouch Versus Press Cells – How Cell Configuration Dictates Low-Pressure Performance in Sulfide Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Sulfide-based solid-state batteries (SSBs) offer the potential for higher energy densities compared to state-of-the-art lithium-ion batteries. Although impressive electrochemical performance has already been demonstrated, these results are predominantly ...

item.title: Trace-State Validation for Agentic Computational Chemistry Workflows
item.description: Agentic chemistry workflows coordinate simulation tools, intermediate records, and automated decisions across agents or agent-like stages. They can fail before any numerical result is produced: a downstream agent or workflow step may act on exchanged ...

item.title: Deciphering the Mechanism and Participation of CF2 carbene as a surrogate in Carbonylative Palladium-Catalyzed Multicomponent Reactions: The Origin of Ligand-
item.description: Difluorocarbene precursors have recently garnered significant attention as versatile C1 synthons for a range of transformations. Among these, carbonylative processes are proposed to proceed via in situ generation of free CO from the difluorocarbene unit, ...

item.title: Engineering Cooperative Multi-Domain DNA Probes for Programmable Nucleic Acid Detection
item.description: Nucleic acids are powerful recognition elements because base pairing enables straightforward probe design, yet selective target recognition under physiological conditions remains challenging when specificity cannot rely on melting-based discrimination. ...

item.title: FFEMR: Foundation Feature-enhanced Molecular Representation for High-Throughput Molecular Optimization
item.description: There is a growing demand for tailormade molecules addressing specific applications, such as green solvents, pharmaceutical ingredients and other functional materials. High-quality chemical encodings are essential for effective molecular optimization to ...

item.title: Soft-templated carbon models elucidate the anomalous increase of capacitance in nanopores
item.description: Supercapacitors are energy storage devices characterized by fast charging capabilities. The discovery of enhanced capacitance in nanoporous carbons radically challenged the understanding of their charging mechanism, sparkling the development of in situ ...

item.title: physcode: A PySCF-Based Gaussian-Style Python Interface for Teaching Quantum Chemistry Beyond the Black Box
item.description: Commercial quantum chemistry packages operate as opaque black boxes for most students. We present physcode, an open-source Python pedagogical interface built around PySCF that reads standard Gaussian-format .gjf input and writes Gaussian-compatible .log ...

item.title: A Compositional Operator Framework for Long-Range Particle Dynamics in Modular Microfluidic Networks
item.description: This work proposes a compositional operator framework in which local exit operators represent particle transport within individual microfluidic modules. The operator maps an encoded representation of the inlet interface state and module context to an exit ...

item.title: Concentration-dependent Transition in the Rate-determining Step of Hydrogen Evolution Driven by Pyridinium-tethered Mononuclear and Dinuclear Platinum Photocatalysts
item.description: Our previous studies on the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) catalyzed by Pt(II)-based molecular catalysts often demonstrated a second-order dependence of the HER rate on the catalyst concentration, suggesting the importance of stabilizing a ...

item.title: Model of the Henrys: Eyring, Frank, and the Foundations for Predicting Transport Coefficients
item.description: Henry Eyring’s absolute rate theory provides a microscopic picture of liquid transport based on molecular jumps, while Henry Frank’s free volume theory relates entropy changes to an effective cage volume in condensed phases. In this work, we integrate ...

item.title: Peroxide-Mediated Pseudo-Current Multiplication: A Predictive Strategy to Enhance Anodic Current in Photoelectrochemical Hydrogen Production
item.description: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) water splitting converts solar energy into hydrogen by coupling anodic water oxidation with cathodic hydrogen evolution. In this framework, the photoanode is not primarily valuable because it produces oxygen; rather, it is ...

item.title: Machine Learning for Predicting the Configuration of Small Molecules
item.description: The configuration of small molecules plays a key role in their measurable properties. This includes their spatial arrangement in 3-dimension space, giving rise to stereoisomers. As different stereoisomers can have vastly different biological effects, its ...

item.title: TopoTunnelMiner : Topology-Guided Mining and Comparative Analysis of Dynamic Protein Tunnels
item.description: The analysis of protein tunnels is essential for elucidating the dynamic variations of substrate entry, product release, and molecular transport pathways. In molecular dynamics simulations, however, continuous conformational motion shifts tunnel ...

item.title: Optimization of pyrazolopyridine derivatives for the treatment of human African trypanosomiasis
item.description: Despite recent advances in the development of therapeutics for human African trypanosomiasis, new treatments are needed to populate the drug pipeline in case of failure and resistance. A previously reported high throughput screen of human kinase ...

item.title: Reversible Acidification Enables Closed-Loop Total Separation of Olivine
item.description: Silicate minerals such as olivine are abundant in mineral deposits and mine tailings, and contain alkalinity, Mg2+, Fe2+, and other trace metals. While historically considered waste, the total processing and separation of these components can generate MgO ...

item.title: Shaping the electric field: Overcoming geometric inhomogeneities in the electrochemical synthesis of mesoporous materials
item.description: (Dated: July 17, 2026) Integrating porous systems such as mesoporous silicon (pSi) into reliable macroscopic devices for sensing, photonics and energy storage requires stringent control over their structural homogeneity. While radial variations in layer ...

item.title: Dual PCET-Enabled Chemoselective Decarboxylative Sulfinylation with N-Sulfinyl Phthalimides as Bench-Stable Sulfinyl Radical Precursors
item.description: Despite their synthetic versatility, sulfinyl radicals have seen limited practical use over six decades, mainly due to the shortage of general and accessible precursors for their controlled generation. Herein, we disclose N-sulfinyl phthalimides as a new ...

item.title: A Generalized Framework Based on Enveloping Distribution Sampling for Calculating Geometrical Free-Energy Differences
item.description: Enveloping distribution sampling (EDS) is an efficient multistate free-energy method that has been mainly applied to estimate alchemical transformations, e.g., for relative solvation or binding free energies. In such simulations, each end-state in the ...

item.title: Configurational Stability of 2,13-Disubstituted [5]Helicenes
item.description: The DFT study performed on a set of 2,13-disubstituted [5]helicenes revealed that phenyl substituents bearing ortho-isopropyl or ortho/meta-tert-butyl groups in particular ensure an increased racemization barrier. Enantioselective synthesis of a ...

item.title: A Geometrical Description of Covalent Bonding Based on the Orbital Generation Method

item.description: Symmetry-lowering orderings in perovskites can produce a wide-range of functions. Thiocyanate based perovskites, AM(NCS)3, have orderings distinct from those of the atomic oxides, enabling new functional behaviour. We report four new defect-ordered ...

item.description: Nanoparticle catalyst properties are often governed by processing-defined near-surface structures rather than nominal bulk phases. Static idealized models therefore can miss the kinetic pathways that drive subsurface evolution and active-site ...

item.title: DeepChem-DEL: Leveraging Chemical Foundation Models to Improve DNA-Encoded Library Modeling
item.description: DNA-encoded libraries (DELs) allow screening of extremely large chemical spaces by tagging compounds with DNA barcodes. As these datasets grow in scale and complexity, machine learning has become essential for signal denoising and binder identification. ...

item.title: Structural Changes are Entangled with Periodic Trends in Thermodynamic Hydricity; Comparing [H–MII(EtXantphos)2]+ and their Hydride Deficient Congeners (M = Ni, Pd, or Pt)
item.description: Experimental measurements of hydricity (ΔGH–) quantify the equilibrium between a metal hydride (H–M) and its hydride-deficient congener (M+). However, periodic trends for transferring H– have proven less predictable than those for H• and H+ amongst 3d/4d/...

item.title: General Applicability of Optical Crystallography to Time-Resolved Serial Xray Crystallography
item.description: Femtosecond laser excitation of molecular crystals results in heterogeneous populations under all conditions of laser power, laser polarization, and crystal symmetry. Crystal optics determine the photoselection and heterogeneous distributions in time-...

item.title: Non-perturbative spin-vibronic reverse intersystem crossing: finite rates through the vibronic resonance and a benchmark on multiresonance delayed-fluorescence emitters
item.description: The reverse intersystem crossing (RISC) that governs delayed fluorescence in organic light-emitting emitters is a spin-vibronic process: spin-orbit coupling between the emissive singlet and a nearby triplet is mediated, at second order, by nonadiabatic ...

item.title: FolioClip: Comprehensive Plant Health Monitoring and Early Stress Detection with Real-Time Multimodal Wearable Sensing and Online Machine Learning
item.description: Wearable plant sensing systems for simultaneous biochemical and physiological monitoring with real-time multimodal data analysis remain limited. Here, we present FolioClip, a multimodal wearable patch that continuously monitors leaf temperature, humidity, ...

item.title: ChemGNN: Graph Neural Network Surrogate Guided Design of Carbon Nanotube Membranes for High-Efficiency Desalination
item.description: ChemGNN, a deep-learning-accelerated framework that learns a graph-based surrogate of membrane transport and actively proposes high-performance designs. Each CNT configuration is encoded as a heterogeneous graph in which tube nodes carry geometry and ...

item.title: Skeletal Editing by formal Fluorocarbyne Insertion: Modular Access to Fluorinated Heterocycles
item.description: Skeletal editing is gaining increasing attention in synthetic chemistry as it enables precise atom-level modifications to reframe molecular structures. However, the single formal carbon-fluorine insertion, which will give access to synthetically ...

item.title: ChemICL: Multimodal In-Context Learning for Accelerated Chemistry Discovery
item.description: Deep learning has transformed chemistry, yet most chemical models remain narrow and task-specific, requiring full retraining for every new target such as molecular property regression, reaction-outcome prediction, or membrane-design ranking. Meanwhile, ...

item.title: Techno-economic Analysis of Biomass-Based Carbon Dioxide Removal Process
item.description: Abstract.Carbon dioxide removal (CDR) processes are a suite of approaches for removing and storing atmospheric CO2. In this work, a techno-economic analysis was performed for a CDR process utilizing low temperature pyrolysis of biomass to sequester ...

item.title: Characterization and Crystal Structure Elucidation of the L-Homoserine O-Succinyltransferase MetXS from Cupriavidus necator H16
item.description: Among the promising microbial platforms to produce value-added products from CO2, chemolithoautotrophic bacteria such asCupriavidus necatorH16 (C. necator) stand out due to their ability to fix CO2via the Calvin-Benson-Bassham (CBB) cycle. AlthoughC. ...

item.title: Robust prediction of chemical equilibria by homologue series
item.description: The prediction of chemical equilibrium requires both a physically sound thermodynamic model and reliable thermodynamic formation properties of the reacting species. In biomass valorization, however, many esterification reactions involve compounds for ...

item.title: Quantification of contaminants and proton exchange in garnet phase powders and their impact on Li+ transport in hybrid electrolytes
item.description: Garnet-phase Li7La3Zr2O12(LLZO) is a promising solid-state electrolyte in lithium metal batteries but suffers from contaminant formation upon exposure to ambient conditions. We use a suite of analytical methods to quantify this contamination and the ...

item.title: Portico: a program for the asymptotic classification of transition-state normal modes
item.description: We present Portico (Projection Onto Roto-Translational Internal COordinates), a program for the automated classification of transition-state normal modes according to their asymptotic product character. In molecular dissociation and fragmentation, some ...

item.title: Stereospecific Engagement of a Methyl-Sensitive ECL2 Anchor Site in 5-HT₂A by α-Substituted NBOH Derivatives
